nodes.label: What lives here (and why it must live here)
nodes.norm_label: what lives here (and why it must live here)

nodes.label: Graphify Skill
nodes.rationale: Turn any folder of files into a navigable knowledge graph with community detection, an honest audit trail, and three outputs: interactive HTML, GraphRAG-ready JSON, and a plain-language GRAPH_REPORT.md.

nodes.label: Fast Path (existing graph)
nodes.rationale: If graphify-out/graph.json exists and the request is a natural-language question about the codebase (not an explicit rebuild command), skip extraction steps entirely and run graphify query directly instead of rebuilding.

nodes.label: Part A: Structural (AST) Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic, free extraction for code files using graphify.extract.extract; runs in parallel with Part B semantic subagents since AST is fast and needs no LLM.

nodes.label: Part B: Semantic Extraction (Parallel Subagents)
nodes.rationale: LLM-based extraction for docs/papers/images via dispatched general-purpose subagents (or a Gemini backend if a key is set); skipped entirely for a code-only corpus.

nodes.label: Step 4: Build Graph, Cluster, Analyze, Generate Outputs
nodes.norm_label: step 4: build graph, cluster, analyze, generate outputs

nodes.label: Step 4.5: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: A non-destructive diagnostic surfacing edge collapse, dangling/missing endpoints, and self-loops - the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches - run before labeling but never aborting.

nodes.label: Step 9: Save Manifest, Update Cost Tracker, Clean Up
nodes.norm_label: step 9: save manifest, update cost tracker, clean up

nodes.label: Interpreter Guard (.graphify_python detection)
nodes.rationale: Find-GraphifyPython resolves the uv/pipx/venv interpreter that actually has graphify installed and saves it so every subsequent step uses the right Python, fixing a class of import failures.

nodes.label: /graphify add <url>
nodes.rationale: Fetches a URL (YouTube, Twitter/X, arXiv, PDF, image, or webpage) via graphify.ingest.ingest, saves it under ./raw, then auto-runs the --update pipeline to merge the new file into the existing graph.

nodes.label: --watch (folder watcher)
nodes.rationale: Background watcher (graphify.watch) that re-runs AST extraction+rebuild immediately for code-only changes, or writes a needs_update flag for docs/papers/images that require LLM semantic re-extraction; debounced so a wave of parallel agent writes doesn't trigger a rebuild per file.

nodes.label: Step 7d: MCP Server
nodes.rationale: Starts a stdio MCP server (graphify.serve) exposing query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, and shortest_path tools so other agents can query the graph live.

nodes.label: Confidence Scoring Rubric
nodes.rationale: A discrete rubric (1.0 EXTRACTED; 0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55 for INFERRED; 0.1-0.3 AMBIGUOUS) exists because models follow discrete rubrics better than continuous ranges - the bimodal distribution observed in production (over 50% at 0.5, over 40% at 0.85+) showed free-form guidance collapsing to a binary.

nodes.label: Node ID Format Rule
nodes.rationale: IDs must be {full_repo_relative_stem}_{entity}, using every directory level and not just the immediate parent, so semantic-extraction IDs deterministically match AST-extractor IDs and never create orphan ghost-duplicate nodes.

nodes.label: graphify merge-graphs
nodes.rationale: Merges multiple graph.json files (cross-repo clones or monorepo subfolders) into one graph; each node carries a repo attribute so results can be filtered by origin.

nodes.label: Monorepo Multi-Subfolder Flow
nodes.rationale: The skill pipeline writes all output to graphify-out/ in the cwd, which would clobber shared output across subfolders, so each subfolder is extracted separately via the CLI (graphify-out/ lands inside the scanned path) and merged at the project root.

nodes.label: graphify hook install/uninstall/status
nodes.rationale: Auto-rebuilds the graph after every git commit by detecting changed code files via git diff HEAD~1 and re-running AST extraction; doc/image changes are ignored by the hook and need a manual --update.

nodes.label: graphify claude install/uninstall
nodes.rationale: Writes a ## graphify section into the project's local CLAUDE.md instructing Claude to check the graph before answering codebase questions and rebuild it after code changes, so graphify is always-on without a manual /graphify invocation.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Expansion (Step 0)
nodes.rationale: graphify query matches via case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language matching, so a wording mismatch between the user's question and the graph's label vocabulary collapses the answer to noise. Fixed by expanding the query against the graph's own extracted vocabulary before traversal, never inventing tokens not present in the graph.

nodes.label: save-result Feedback / Work Memory
nodes.rationale: Closes a self-improving feedback loop: --outcome useful|dead_end|corrected marks cited nodes as preferred sources, dead ends, or corrections so future --update runs and graphify reflect surface accumulated lessons.

nodes.label: Whisper Domain-Hint Prompt Step
nodes.rationale: The host LLM writes its own one-sentence domain hint from god-node labels and passes it to Whisper as the initial prompt instead of a separate API call, improving transcription accuracy for domain vocabulary.

nodes.label: graphify.build.build_merge
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction (calls/implements/imports) is always preserved; replace-on-re-extract drops every source_file present in the new chunks from the base before merging, so changed files don't leave stale duplicate nodes behind.

nodes.label: TRMission (台鐵任務)
nodes.rationale: A multiplayer web board game: a clean-room reimplementation of the mechanics of Ticket to Ride: Europe, re-themed onto Taiwan's railways. The map, city graph, artwork, palette, and rules wording are original; only the mechanics and real Taiwanese place-names are reused.

nodes.label: @trm/engine Pure Deterministic Reducer
nodes.rationale: reduce(state, action) -> { state, events } with zero I/O. Determinism is enforced structurally: @trm/engine is ESLint-banned from Date, Math.random, crypto.randomUUID, and new Date(); all randomness comes from the seeded counter PRNG. Golden-replay digests are a CI gate.

nodes.label: redactFor / Hidden Information Structural Design
nodes.rationale: Opponents are a counts-only wire type (PublicPlayerState); a viewer's secrets live in a disjoint SelfView. The single redactFor projection is the only thing that should ever reach the wire, backed by a runtime egress guard and the trm_security_leak_blocked_total metric - raw GameState must never be serialized to a client.

nodes.label: apps/server/src/ws/hub.ts (hub)
nodes.rationale: The critical loop: decode -> serialize through a per-game queue -> write-ahead persist -> commit on the engine -> fan out a per-recipient redacted snapshot.

nodes.label: swc, not tsx (tooling pin)
nodes.rationale: NestJS DI resolves constructor deps from emitted decorator metadata, which esbuild/tsx does not produce. Server dev/start run via @swc-node/register, vitest uses unplugin-swc - switching to tsx/esbuild silently breaks DI at runtime.

nodes.label: Single-Writer Per Game
nodes.rationale: A per-game command queue serializes decode->validate->apply->persist->fan-out; a unique (gameId, seq) Mongo index is the durable double-apply guard; idempotency is on the monotonic per-socket client_seq.

nodes.label: Version Pins (engineVersion/contentHash/schemaVersion)
nodes.rationale: Stamped on persisted games; replay refuses to cross versions. CONTENT_HASH is derived from the authored map-data content, so any map edit changes it.

nodes.label: Naming/Tooling Pins (PURPLE, seat indices, Vite ^5)
nodes.rationale: The 6th card colour is PURPLE everywhere (never PINK); seat colours are abstract indices 0-4 on the wire, coloured client-side; apps/web pins Vite ^5 for vitest 2 compatibility - do not bump to Vite 6.

nodes.label: graphify Knowledge Graph Integration
nodes.rationale: The project has a knowledge graph at graphify-out/ with god nodes, community structure, and cross-file relationships; codebase questions should run graphify query/path/explain before falling back to GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep.

nodes.norm_label: readme: auth (guest, argon2id, hs256, refresh rotation)

nodes.label: Task 2: Wire SpotlightFramer to frameDurationMs
nodes.rationale: A mechanical, behavior-neutral substitution for existing callers: every current frameTarget caller (tutorial, encyclopedia) never sets instant, so frameDurationMs returns exactly what the inline expression returned before.

nodes.label: animate flag (useReplayPlayer)
nodes.rationale: True immediately after a next()-driven step, false after any applyTo()-driven change (seek, prev, setViewer, initial mount) - the glide-vs-snap signal for the replay camera-follow.

nodes.label: followActing (store/ui.ts)
nodes.rationale: The eye-icon toggle shared with live play; ReplayStage sets it true once on mount so replay always opens with follow active, and the user can still turn it off.
